snippet.definition:
//ˈsnɪpɪt//
noun
  1. A small part of something, such as a song or fabric; sample.
  2. A text file containing a relatively small amount of code, useless by itself, along with instructions for inserting that code into a larger codebase.
  e.g. From the snippet I heard of their rehearsal, they sound pretty good.
verb
  1. To produce a snippet (small part) of; to excerpt.
  2. To make small cuts, to snip, particularly with scissors.
  e.g. We snippeted the blog posts for display on the home page.

definitions.example: From the snippet I heard of their rehearsal, they sound pretty good.
meanings: A small part of something, such as a song or fabric; sample.
A text file containing a relatively small amount of code, useless by itself, along with instructions for inserting that code into a larger codebase.

definitions.example: We snippeted the blog posts for display on the home page.
meanings: To produce a snippet (small part) of; to excerpt.
To make small cuts, to snip, particularly with scissors.

snippet.sourceTitle: Inside vLLM: Anatomy of a High-Throughput LLM Inference System | vLLM Blog
